sprint: Zero-Chrome Fix Was Being Defeated By a Dangling Frame Variation Reference
task: A direct, same-day follow-up: the user reported still seeing solid white Artwork Place boxes on their real 'My shapes' World after the previous fix shipped (confirmed deployed via the visible build-info footer). Root-caused by walking the user through a live browser-console dump of their real project's slide.metadata rather than guessing further: slide.metadata.cardOverrides.artwork.frameVariation was the string 'classic', but the active theme's frameVariations was null -- a stale, dangling seed from an earlier Builder state (a Frame the Theme Author has since renamed or removed) that survived on this already-existing Creator project. The previous fix's _hasExplicitChrome flag only checked whether SOME frameVariation id string was present (!!frameVariationId), not whether it actually resolved to a real entry in theme.frameVariations -- so a dangling reference kept tricking Creator into treating the Place as 'explicitly Framed' and falling back to the Presentation Preset's white System Default, exactly reproducing the reported symptom. Fixed by tracking whether the Frame Variation lookup genuinely found a matching entry with real fields (frameVariationResolved) and basing _hasExplicitChrome on that instead of the bare id string's truthiness -- a stale/unresolvable reference now correctly falls through to zero chrome, matching a Place that never had a Frame Variation at all. Verified by reproducing the user's exact real-project state (a real Build via ProjectCompiler, then stripping frameVariations off the live theme record and seeding the identical dangling frameVariation:'classic' reference onto the slide): Place 1's edge pixel now reads the theme's wall tone (234,231,226), not solid white (255,255,255). Re-verified the prior fix's own navy Scene-background scenario and Museum Gallery's real classic-white Frame Variation are both still correct and unaffected. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
